text: And. Notwithstanding the sins of his people, God will deliver them, that they may be his witnesses, ver. 10. (Calmet)<hr>Name. As an intimate friend, (Haydock) or slave, Isaias 49:1. (Calmet)<hr>God grants grace without any preceding merit. (Worthington)<hr>Forerius explains this mostly of the Gentile church, though it regards the converted Jews. (Houbigant)

Waters. In every emergency, God will protect thee, (Calmet) as he did the three children. [Daniel iii.] (Haydock)<hr>He will suffer none to perish, without their own fault. Hence the Church still continues pure. (Worthington)

Ethiopia. Hebrew, "Chus," the isle of Meroe, (Grotius) or the country bordering on the Red Sea, Isaias 45:14. These were given to indemnify Cyrus. They obtained their liberty to return home, after 40 years, Ezechiel 29:11.
Eyes, by a gratuitous choice.<hr>Men. Chaldeans, etc.
East. Babylon.<hr>West. The islands beyond the Mediterranean.
North. Assyria.<hr>South. Egypt. The captives returned from these countries under Cyrus, Alexander the Great, etc., so that before the coming of Christ, Judea was as well peopled as ever. (Calmet)
Calleth. Hebrew, "is called by my name." (Protestants) (Haydock)<hr>My people's chastisements and liberation prove my divine perfections.
Ears. Yet will not hear, (Calmet) or who are miraculously healed. (Haydock)<hr>It seems rather to mean idolatrous nations, (ver. 9.; Calmet) or rebellious Jews. (Forerius) (Houbigant)
Former. The pagans were little acquainted with antiquity. Such researches lead to the knowledge of the true religion. (Haydock)<hr>Truth, if they can produce any true prophecy.

Witnesses. The history of the Israelites was sufficient to shew who was God. (Calmet)<hr>Thus the establishment of Christianity manifests its truth. (St. Augustine, City of God 22:6.)<hr>Septuagint add, "and I am witness." (Haydock)
One idol, to announce what would happen.

Bars. Septuagint, "fugitives." Theodotion, "strong ones."<hr>Glorying. Septuagint, "shall be bound in ships," to be sent beyond the Caspian Sea. (Calmet)<hr>Cyrus was victorious for the sake of God's people; for he will not neglect his Church. (Worthington)
Waters of the Jordan and the Red Sea, in the latter of which Pharao perished, ver. 17.
New. I shall work the like miracles as were seen in the wilderness.

Chosen. We know not that rivers were found in Arabia. But the people were equally favoured. Christ facilitates the road to heaven by his example and graces, while the most savage tempers are changed in baptism.
Incense. My kindness is gratuitous. In a strange land, thou couldst not offer sacrifice. See Isaias 48:9., and 49:1. (Calmet)
Iniquities. Thou hast shewn the greatest ingratitude. (Haydock)<hr>Yet I will save thee.
Thyself. God condescends to act thus with men, Isaias 41:20., and Osee 4:1.

First father. Adam, (Lyranus) or rather Abraham sinned, by diffidence, (Genesis 15:8.; St. Jerome) or was formerly an idolater, Josue 24:2. (Genebrard, the year of the world 2049) (St. Augustine, City of God 16:12.) (Calmet) (Tirinus)<hr>Teachers. Literally, "interpreters," (Haydock) Moses and Aaron, Numbers 20:9. (Calmet)<hr>All the patriarchs and teachers sinned, till Christ, the immaculate lamb, appeared. Adam engaged all in guilt. (Worthington)

Profaned, or declared such, (Haydock) Nadab, etc., (Leviticus 10:1.; Calmet) or Moses and Aaron. (Menochius)<hr>Septuagint, "and the princes defiled my holy things." (Haydock)<hr>Slaughter. Hebrew, "anathema." Yet I will re-establish all. (Calmet)
